get-self-test-kit-for-HIV.title: Get a self-test kit for HIV
get-self-test-kit-for-HIV.ageRequirement: You can use this service if you are aged 18 or over.
get-self-test-kit-for-HIV.availabilityNotice: The kit is not yet available in all areas, so we'll need to check when you enter a postcode.

urgentCard.heading: Go to a sexual health clinic if:
urgentCard.exposureWarning: you think you've been exposed to the HIV virus in the last 72 hours
urgentCard.clinicAdvice: Clinic staff can offer you emergency HIV medicine, which may stop you getting infected.
urgentCard.aeAdvice: You can also get emergency HIV medicine from your

infoBox.text: HIV antibodies take time to develop. The test is accurate if you take it 45 days or more after potential exposure.

howItWorks.heading: How it works
howItWorks.deliveryInfo: The kit will be delivered in plain packaging, to the address you choose, within 5 working days. This does not have to be your usual address.
howItWorks.sampleInstructions: You'll need to prick your finger and fill a tube with blood. Then send it to the address in the pre-paid envelope.

dataSharing.summary: Who my information is shared with
dataSharing.details: Your contact details will only be shared with the kit supplier, who is a trusted NHS partner. Your contact details and test result will not be shared with anyone else, including your GP, unless you give consent.

aboutService.heading: About using this service
aboutService.text: By using this service, you agree to our

otherOptions.heading: Other options to home testing, and more support
otherOptions.clinicText: Instead of doing the test at home, you can go to
otherOptions.sexualHealthText: If you're concerned about other sexual health issues, you can

sexualHealthLink.text: find sexual health services near you

kit-not-available-in-area.title: Free HIV self-test kits are not available in your area using this service
kit-not-available-in-area.description: There are other options to get an HIV test.

how-comfortable-pricking-finger.title: This is what you'll need to do to give a blood sample
how-comfortable-pricking-finger.instructions: To give a sample of blood, you'll need to:

image.alt: Person collecting blood sample by pricking finger, with blood droplet falling into collection tube.

how-comfortable-pricking-finger.formLabel: Are you comfortable doing the HIV self-test?

yes.text: Yes I'm comfortable, send me the kit
yes.hint: The test is supplied by {supplier}, a trusted partner of the NHS

no.text: No, I'd rather go to a sexual health clinic instead
no.hint: They will take a blood sample for you

negativeResult.summary: A negative test result means the test did not detect HIV.
negativeResult.windowPeriodAdvice: However, if you took the test less than 45 days after potential exposure, wait until the 45 days have passed and do a new test to confirm the negative result. This is because HIV takes time to show in a blood sample.
negativeResult.contactSupplierPrefix: If you have any questions about your result,
negativeResult.gpSharing: We do not share your test result with your GP.
negativeResult.nextStepsAdvice: We recommend you do an HIV test every 3 months if you regularly have sex without condoms with new or casual partners.

moreOptionsAndInformation.heading: More options and information
moreOptionsAndInformation.fullCheckPrefix: For a full sexual health check for HIV, Chlamydia, Gonorrhoea, and Syphilis,
moreOptionsAndInformation.fullCheckLink: complete an online sexual health questionnaire with {supplier}
moreOptionsAndInformation.prepAdvice: reduces the risk of getting HIV. You can get PrEP from a health professional at an
moreOptionsAndInformation.learnMoreHIV: Learn more about HIV and AIDS

medicalAbbreviationsHelp.intro: You may see medical abbreviations you are not familiar with.

image.alt: Image showing the contents of the kit
image.caption: Kit contents.

tipsBeforeStart.intro: These tips can help you collect your blood sample:
tips: Drink a big glass of water at least 30 minutes before you do the test
Do the test after a shower or bath
Put your hand into a bowl of warm water a few minutes before doing the test
Swing your arms in a circular motion. Or clench your hands into a fist a few times
Read the instructions in full before starting

steps.heading: Step 1: Get ready
steps.caption: Wash your hands using warm water and soap. Dry your hands. Set out everything from your test kit on a clean, flat surface. Take the lid off the blood sample tube. Stand the tube upright so it's ready.

steps.heading: Step 2: Prepare your finger
steps.caption: Choose a finger from the hand you don't write with (your ring finger or little finger is best). Open the alcohol wipe. Rub your chosen fingertip with the wipe. Wait for your finger to air dry.

steps.heading: Step 3: Prick your finger
steps.caption: Remove the cap from the lancet. Push the lancet firmly onto the side of your fingertip. You'll hear a small click. Use a tissue to dab off the first drop of blood.

steps.heading: Step 4: Collect the blood

image.alt: Person dripping blood into a sample tube

steps.caption: Keep your hand hanging down below waist level. Gently massage your finger starting from the bottom and moving toward the tip. Blood drops will fall into the sample tube.

steps.heading: Step 5: Fill the tube

image.alt: Blood sample tube filled with blood

steps.caption: Aim to fill the tube to the top line marked 600. If you can only get to 400, send it back anyway. There are more lancets in your kit if you need to prick another finger.

steps.heading: Step 6: Seal and mix the tube

image.alt: Person turning tube upside down 5 times

steps.caption: Hold a clean tissue or cotton pad against your fingertip. Place the plaster over the spot. Put the yellow lid back on the tube and push it firmly until it clicks. Turn the tube upside down and back up 5 times to mix the blood well.

steps.heading: Step 7: Pack and post
steps.caption: Place your blood sample tube into the protective packaging following the kit instructions. Write today's date and the time on the form. Put the form and the sealed bag into the pre-paid postage box. Seal it with the security sticker. Put your sample in a post box as soon as you can.
